allEffects.name: Empathy, affection and sociability enhancement
allEffects.notes: The signature entactogenic effect — feeling open, warm, and emotionally connected to others.

allEffects.name: Euphoria
allEffects.notes: Intense sense of happiness and well-being, especially in social settings.

allEffects.name: Increased music appreciation
allEffects.notes: Music sounds incredible. Many users report music becoming the main focus of the experience.

allEffects.name: Energy
allEffects.notes: Stimulant-like energy, especially at higher doses. Can keep you dancing for hours.

allEffects.name: Openness
allEffects.notes: Easier to talk to strangers, express emotions, be yourself without social anxiety.

allEffects.name: Emotionality
allEffects.notes: Deep feelings arise — can be joyful crying, meaningful conversations, or surfacing past emotions.

allEffects.name: Increased salivation
allEffects.notes: Jaw clenching is common. Keep gum or a pacifier handy.

allEffects.name: Muscle tension
allEffects.notes: Jaw clenching, teeth grinding, body tension. Very common at moderate-to-high doses.

allEffects.name: Perspiration
allEffects.notes: You'll sweat. Take breaks, drink water, stay cool.

allEffects.name: Appetite suppression
allEffects.notes: Food has no appeal during the experience. Forcing yourself to eat usually ends badly.

allEffects.name: Nausea
allEffects.notes: Common during come-up, especially with pressed pills of unknown purity. Usually passes within 30 min.

allEffects.name: Insomnia
allEffects.notes: Sleep is very difficult for 4-6 hours after peak. Plan accordingly.

allEffects.name: Dilated pupils
allEffects.notes: Light sensitivity is common.

allEffects.name: Restlessness
allEffects.notes: Impulse to move constantly. Can be channeled into dancing.

allEffects.name: Increased heart rate
allEffects.notes: Cardiovascular strain is real. Watch your dose and your BPM.

allEffects.name: Depression (afterglow / comedown)
allEffects.notes: 'Tuesday blues' — low mood 1-3 days after. Usually resolves with rest.

allEffects.name: Anxiety
allEffects.notes: Can hit during come-up or if you think too hard about the experience. Set and setting matter.

allEffects.name: Visual distortion
allEffects.notes: Mild compared to psychedelics. Enhanced colors, patterns on surfaces, tracers at higher doses.

allEffects.name: Time distortion
allEffects.notes: Time can feel stretched. Songs feel longer than they are.

allEffects.name: Dehydration
allEffects.notes: MDMA raises body temperature. Dancers need water. Overhydration is also dangerous — drink to thirst.

allEffects.name: Body load
allEffects.notes: Feeling heavy, sluggish, or nauseated in the body — especially during come-up and comedown.

allEffects.name: Memory suppression
allEffects.notes: Short-term memory can be impaired during peak. Don't expect to remember conversations.

allEffects.name: Libido increase
allEffects.notes: Touch feels amazing. Emotional intimacy often increases more than sexual desire.

allEffects.name: Compulsive redosing
allEffects.notes: The urge to take more is strong. Resist — it increases neurotoxicity risk and ruins the next day.

allEffects.name: Hyperthermia
allEffects.notes: Medical emergency. Can be fatal. Keep cool, stay hydrated, take breaks.

mostLoved: Music empathy (feeling the emotion in music)

timeline.description: First signs appear. May feel nervous, jittery, or slightly nauseous. Body feels different — warm, tingly. Anxiety is common but usually passes.
The wave hits. Resistance gives way to an overwhelming sense of warmth, openness, and connection. Music starts to sound different.
Full effects. Everything feels right with the world. Empathy is sky-high. Conversations become deep. Dancing is effortless. You're here with your people.
Effects stabilize at a high plateau. Still rolling but intensity eases slightly. This is the sweet spot for conversations and dancing.
Effects gradually decline. May feel tired, emotionally raw, or depleted. The 'lovely lingering' period where you're content but energy fades.
24-72 hours after: possible fatigue, low mood, difficulty concentrating. 'Tuesday blues' peaks around day 2-3. Full recovery by day 5-7.

whyUsersLikeIt.summary: MDMA is the closest thing to a drug that makes you a better version of yourself — more open, more connected, more alive. It doesnu2019t just make you feel good; it makes you feel close. Thatu2019s why itu2019s dominated dance music culture for 40 years and is now showing up in therapy sessions. The magic is real, but it comes with a cost: your brain chemistry doesnu2019t bounce back as fast as you want it to. Three months between rolls isnu2019t hippie advice u2014 itu2019s the minimum your neurons need.

reasons.description: MDMA is famous for obliterating social anxiety. You can walk up to anyone, say anything, feel everything. People who struggle with social connection find it opens a door they normally keep locked.
reasons.sourcePattern: Patterns from Erowid: 'felt like I could finally say what I actually feel'; PsychonautWiki: 'significant enhancement of empathy and sociability'; Reddit r/MDMA: 'I finally told my dad I love him after 20 years'

reasons.description: MDMA makes music sound transcendent. Not distorted or hallucinatory — enhanced. Basslines hit differently. Vocals feel like they're inside your chest. This is why it survived as the EDM drug of choice across multiple decades and genres.
reasons.sourcePattern: Erowid reports consistently cite music as a top effect; Reddit: 'I have never cried to a song until I rolled'; 'The music IS the experience at a festival on MDMA'

reasons.description: Pure, clean euphoria that's different from stimulants or opioids. It's warm, it's social, it's emotionally productive. You're not checked out — you're dialed in to what's around you, but everything feels good.
reasons.sourcePattern: PsychonautWiki: 'intense feelings of euphoria, contentment, and warmth'; Reddit: 'not a drug high, a human high'; Erowid: 'the best feeling I know'

reasons.description: MDMA at a music festival or club is basically a cheat code for connection. You're dancing, everyone around you is also rolling or has rolled before, the music is perfect, and the sense of collective joy is palpable. This is the dominant street use case.
reasons.sourcePattern: Universal festival crowd experience; Reddit: 'saw my favorite artist rolling — it was a religious experience'; 'the crowd energy on molly hits different'

reasons.description: In clinical settings (MAPS trials, therapy sessions), MDMA is used to access difficult emotions with less fear. Users report processing trauma, deepening relationships, and gaining insights. Street equivalent: late-night deep conversations with someone you trust.
reasons.sourcePattern: MAPS Phase 3 trials: 'significant reduction in PTSD symptoms'; PsychonautWiki: 'enhanced introspection and access to suppressed emotions'; Reddit: 'I finally processed grief I had been holding for years'

reasons.description: Touch feels incredible on MDMA. Hugs land differently. Rubbing your hands on fabric is satisfying. Massages are transcendent. Skin is more sensitive. This is a big part of why it's called an entactogen ('producing a touching within').
reasons.sourcePattern: PsychonautWiki: 'tactile enhancement is pronounced'; Reddit: 'I couldn't stop touching my friends arm'; 'every hug felt like a warm blanket'; 'the pacifier saved my jaw but I also just loved having something in my mouth'

reasons.description: At lower doses (light to common range), MDMA can be used for focused creative work, writing, or social events where you need energy without psychedelia. Less common but real.
reasons.sourcePattern: PsychonautWiki notes 'wakefulness and stimulation' at moderate doses; Reddit: 'microdosed MDMA for a conference'; 'used it to get through a networking event'

useCases.description: The classic. Take 1.5-2 hours before gates open. Effects peak right around the headliner. Stay cool, stay hydrated, take breaks from the crowd. The collective roll during a great set is one of the best experiences in electronic music culture.

Similar to festival but tighter crowd, smaller space. MDMA makes every room feel special. The intimacy of a basement club with good sound on MDMA is a different kind of magic — more intense, more personal.

Used with a trusted partner, friend, or in a clinical setting. Allows access to emotions without the normal fear response. Used for couples therapy, grief processing, and PTSD work. Not for casual use — needs intention and safety.
MDMA with one person you trust. Deep conversations, meaningful touch, emotional closeness. Much more about intimacy and connection than sexuality. Many couples use it to reconnect.
Less common but real. Low doses can enhance music listening alone, creative work, journaling, or meditation. Not recommended for beginners — the social component is often key to the experience.

House party, wedding, networking event. MDMA makes you the most popular person in the room. You're warm, funny, engaged, and people feel good around you. Risk: you might tell people things you regret on Tuesday.

streetQuotes.text: The warmth was incredible. I hugged my best friend for what felt like an hour. We both cried and laughed and talked about everything we'd been scared to say. This is what I take MDMA for.
MDMA produces entactogenic effects described as feeling of mental clarity and emotional closeness to others. Music is experienced as unusually profound and meaningful.

I've done molly 50+ times at festivals. Nothing beats that moment when the music hits and 10,000 people around you are all feeling the exact same thing. It's not a drug thing, it's a community thing that molly unlocked.
The come-up anxiety is real. First 20 minutes I always think 'is this not working?' or 'am I doing something wrong?' Then it hits and it's the smoothest transition into a different headspace I know.
The best roll I ever had was at a small house party with 10 people I loved. No festival, no big crowd. Just good music, good friends, and enough MDMA to feel everything without losing myself.

Every three months minimum. I learned the hard way. Did it two weeks apart once and the second time was nothing — just anxious, overheated, and sad. Gave it 4 months and it came back better than ever.
Reagent tested my molly before rolling. Was glad I did. Someone else's batch that night at the same venuehospitalized two people. Test your shit.
